Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 248 upvotes, 26 comments
  2. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 168 upvotes, 31 comments
  3. Building a new Flash 535 upvotes, 155 comments
  4. You Just Reveived 158 upvotes, 40 comments
  5. “It turns out” (2010) 280 upvotes, 88 comments
  6. Smalltalk's Browser: Unbeatable, yet Not Enough 23 upvotes, 3 comments
  7. Google Workspace CLI 559 upvotes, 195 comments
  8. Arabic document from 17th-cent. rubbish heap confirms semi-legendary Nubian king 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. Show HN: Poppy – A simple app to stay intentional with relationships 83 upvotes, 24 comments
  10. No right to relicense this project 73 upvotes, 22 comments
  11. Dbslice: Extract a slice of your production database to reproduce bugs 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  12. Noem Can't Explain Why She Hired 8-Day-Old Company for Ad Campaign 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  13. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 661 upvotes, 296 comments
  14. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 114 upvotes, 42 comments
  15. Chaos and Dystopian news for the dead internet survivors 96 upvotes, 36 comments
  16. The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying 122 upvotes, 53 comments
  17. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 560 upvotes, 301 comments
  18. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 116 upvotes, 53 comments
  19. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 62 upvotes, 25 comments
  20. Dulce et Decorum Est (1921) 129 upvotes, 70 comments
  21. Jails for NetBSD – Kernel Enforced Isolation and Native Resource Control 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  22. Relax NG is a schema language for XML (2014) 23 upvotes, 9 comments
  23. NRC issues first commercial reactor construction approval in 10 years [pdf] 107 upvotes, 71 comments
  24. What Python’s asyncio primitives get wrong about shared state 53 upvotes, 31 comments
  25. AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 67 upvotes, 48 comments
  26. US tech firms pledge at White House to bear costs of energy for datacenters 69 upvotes, 52 comments
  27. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 92 upvotes, 74 comments
  28. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 127 upvotes, 112 comments
  29. The Self-Help Trap: What 20 Years of "Optimizing" Has Taught Me 45 upvotes, 36 comments
  30. MacBook Neo 1777 upvotes, 2079 comments

Last generated 5:30pm, 5 March 2026. Source here.
